summary: Process/documentation PR that added a GitHub pull request template pointing contributors to the contributing guidelines, and expanded `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` with explicit AI assistance rules for decomp work. The new guidance allows AI for function/code matching but sets boundaries around naming, data-section matching, PR/comment content, and automated reviews.
what_changed: Documented that using AI to match functions is acceptable and frequently practiced.
Added guidance to avoid letting AI name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.
Added guidance to discourage AI from attempting to match data sections, keeping AI use focused on code matches.

Added guidance to avoid including AI output in PR bodies, issues, comments, or automated code reviews; concise relevant AI summaries may be included only when visually differentiated with markdown quote formatting.
Added `.github/pull_request_template.md` with a one-line reminder to read the contributing guidelines before submitting a PR.
smart_moves: Moved AI policy into contributor documentation instead of relying on informal review norms.
Used the PR template as a lightweight enforcement mechanism so future contributors see the contributing guidelines before opening PRs.
Separated acceptable AI use for function/code matching from higher-risk areas like public symbol naming and data-section matching.
Protected review signal quality by discouraging raw AI output, automated code reviews, and noisy PR/comment content.
decomp_lessons: AI assistance may be useful for matching functions, but decomp projects should keep human control over globally visible names.
Struct field names and other global identifiers have long-term project-wide consequences, so they should not be delegated to AI-generated guesses.
Data-section matching is called out as an area where AI should be discouraged; focus AI assistance on code matches instead.
PR communication should stay concise and human-readable; AI-generated summaries, if used, should be clearly differentiated from human writing.
naming_conventions: Do not allow AI to name fields in structs.
Do not allow AI to name other globally accessible identifiers.
The policy implies globally visible names should be chosen by humans based on project conventions and evidence, not AI guesses.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: AI use is explicitly permitted for matching functions/code.
AI should be discouraged from attempting to match data sections.
No specific assembly, ordering, register-allocation, or compiler-matching tactic was included in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or review comments referencing the AI Assistance guidelines.
Search for review feedback involving AI-generated struct field names or global identifiers.
Search for policy changes to `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` or `.github/pull_request_template.md` after PR 2513.
Look for later discussions about AI-assisted data-section matching.
